memoryArtifacts.headline: The Spencer Dynasty
memoryArtifacts.description:
The origins of the Spencer name come from the 11th Century A.D. in Norman England, after the conquest of England by William the
Conquerer in 1066. One of the Norman knights, who accompanied William's …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hawise le Despenser於1344年出生在英國林可爾史爾德尼時，她的父親Philip le Despenser31歲，母親Joan Cobham28歲。她於1363年9月7日與Sir Andrew Luttrell在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾易爾納姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1414年4月10日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾易爾納姆享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾易爾納姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hawise le Despenser于1344年出生在英国林可爾史爾德尼时，她的父亲Philip le Despenser31岁，母亲Joan Cobham28岁。她于1363年9月7日与Sir Andrew Luttrell在英国英格兰林可爾史爾易爾納姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1414年4月10日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾易爾納姆享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾易爾納姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
meanings: This female name (which has been corrupted down to Avice and Avis) is from Hadewisa, formed from the German name Hedvig, same as Edwig, which Wachter translates 'propugnator felicitatis;' say, 'fortunate soldier.'

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Hawise le Despenser was born in 1344, in Gedney, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Philip le Despenser, was 31 and her mother, Joan Cobham, was 28. She married Sir Andrew Luttrell on 7 September 1363, in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 10 April 1414, in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hawise le Despenser est née en 1344, à Gedney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip le Despenser, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Joan Cobham, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Andrew Luttrell le 7 septembre 1363, à Irnham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 10 avril 1414, à Irnham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Irnham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hawise le Despenser 1344 in Gedney, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philip le Despenser 31 und ihre Mutter Joan Cobham 28. Sie hat am 7. September 1363 in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Andrew Luttrell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 10. April 1414 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Irnham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hawise le Despenser è nata il nel 1344 nel Gedney, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Philip le Despenser, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Joan Cobham, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Sir Andrew Luttrell il 7 settembre 1363, nel Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 10 aprile 1414, nel Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hawise le Despenserが1344年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアジェドニーで生まれた時，父Philip le Despenserは31歳，母Joan Cobhamは28歳であった。1363年9月7日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアイルンハムでSir Andrew Luttrellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1414年4月10日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアイルンハムで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアイルンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hawise le Despenser(이)가 1344년 영국 링코실르 게드니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip le Despenser(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Cobham(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1363년 9월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 언함에서 Sir Andrew Luttrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1414년 4월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 언함에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 언함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hawise le Despenser nasceu em 1344, em Gedney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip le Despenser, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Joan Cobham, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Andrew Luttrell em 7 de setembro de 1363, em Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 10 de abril de 1414, em Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hawise le Despenser родилась в 1344 г. в местности джедни, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Philip le Despenser было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Cobham, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Andrew Luttrell 7 сентября 1363 г. в местности ирнам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 10 апреля 1414 г. в местности ирнам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности ирнам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hawise le Despenser nació en 1344, en Gedney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip le Despenser, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Joan Cobham, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir Andrew Luttrell el 7 de septiembre de 1363, en Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 10 de abril de 1414, en Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Irnham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
